preamble: The voiceless velar plosive [k] shifted to the glottal fricative [h] (via [x]) in Germanic languages.

examples.note: In Enga (Trans-New Guinea, Enga Province, Papua New Guinea), the voiceless velar stop /k/ is realized as the velar fricative [x] or further debuccalized to [h] when it appears between low and back vowels (e.g., /a_a/, /o_o/ environments). This allophonic or incipient sound change represents the debuccalization path k → x → h characteristic of many Papuan and world languages.
